properties.remark: Gene Autry and Vista Chino at the wash closed due to blowing sand and reduced visibility.
Large boulders blocking lanes on South Grade Road.
CalTrans report of a rock slide in upward-bound lanes of Lytle Creek Road.
Rockslides on Ortega highway 74 at county line. 0.80 inches of rain since early morning.
SR189 and Village Rd rockslide in lanes.
All lanes flooded on I-5 at Pershing (b St). 1.38 inch past 1 hour in National City EW3219. Vehicle stuck in lane 1. 1.11 at station 7459 past 1 hour.
Mudslide I805 N Imperial Ave off ramp.
3741 Sweetwater Rd EUCALYPTUS TREE BLOCKING ALL LNS - wind gusts to 30-35 mph.
Trained spotter report of 6 inches of water flowing over roadway over a width of 30 feet. Water is up to the bumpers of cars.
I5 N and 28th street on ramp, vehicle stuck in water and persons in vehicle. Rainfall of 1.30 inches in past hour in National City.
SR 163 S and Ash St Off ramp with major flooding. 0.80 to 1.30 inches past 1 hour.
Mesonet station XIMP Imperial Beach Pier (WXFLOW).
I5 S and 28th (national) - vehicles flooding VEHICLES FLOATING IN THE WATER ON SB 5 NEAR BASE ENTRANCE.
I-805 S / Imperial Ave significant flooding. This area had rainfall rates of 1 inch per hour and 2-3 inches in 3 hours.
Entire freeway flooded. SR 94 W and I-805 - rainfall of 2 to 3 inches past 3 hours.
Vehicle has 3 to 4 feet of water over it at I805 N and Imperial Ave offramp. 2 to 3 inches of rain past 3 hours.
WB SR-78 all lanes closed from College Blvd to El Camino Real due to flooding.
2 trees down on lanes at SR163 S and Washington St W Offramp.
Mudslide SR94 Barrett School Rd.
I805 North and El Cajon North all 4 lanes flooded.
I-5 south and Main_division South (National City) at least 2 vehicles in water. This area received 2 to 3 inches of rain in 3 hours.
Public report from social media shows pictures of flooding on roads near Ocean Beach dog beach near Spray Avenue. Flood waters up to half length of parked car tires.
Public report from social media shows pictures of flooding from Chollas Creek on Rigel Road. Flood waters shown depict cars submerged up to handles of side doors.

San Diego River reached 11.3 feet at USGS Fashion Mall gauge. New flood stage is 10 feet. Flooding in the area was significant including full closure and barricades with flowing water over all crossings, Qualcomm Way, Mission Center road, Fashion Valley road, Camino del Este, Ward road, and Mission road. San Diego fire reported 25 water rescues in the region and tributaries. The Fashion Valley parking garage had 2 to 3 feet of water in the lowest level. The Ward and Fashion Valley trolly centers were surrounded by 1 to 3 feet of water. Water was flowing up to the Riverleaf Inn, mostly blocked by K-rail but was flowing into Hotel circle. Flooded areas were observed as early as 1 pm. Flash flooding along the river including Ward road.
CalTrans San Diego reports all lanes of northbound Route 15 closed south of Ocean View Blvd due to flooding.
CalTrans reports Hawthorn Street off-ramp of Interstate 5 closed due to flooding. This ramp is used by a high volume of traffic to get to the San Diego Airport.
Social media video shows flooded parking lot with a river of water along the train tracks in Encanto. The strong river of water carried away a car, which had wipers actively engaged.
Orange County Fire rescues a person from flood waters at Sunflower Avenue and Fairview Road. Social media video shows flood water rushing through a channel.

Pacific Coast Highway both lanes flooded out. Some impact to businesses with significant flooding reported. 1.8 to 2.65 inches in Seal Beach with much of this 1.65 to 1.95 inches in 2 hours from 9 am to 11 am. Flooding into residents property and garages on 7th and 8th south of Marina Drive with streets full of water 12 inches at least.
SR 330 rockslide - large boulders in downhill lane - 3.6 inches of rain past 24 hours.
Boulder 4 feet wide on roadway. Hellers Bnd and South Mission Rd. 2.5 inches of rain far.
Woodside ave and Riverwood road, rocks falling off hillside. Along San Diego River.
Rocks and debris on road at SR94 and Otay Lakes road.
Multiple rocks on SR 18 Arctic circle at 7000 feet. This area received 1 to 2 inches of precipitation. Also 2 inches of very wet snowfall.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 3 ESE Riverside: Tree down, 4 ft diameter Italian Stone Pine between 8 and 10 am. Root failure in wet soil. Small car crushed.
Tree down. 4 ft diameter Italian Stone Pine.
Mud and rock slide on the southbound lane of Highway 18. Rain total through 2 PM was 0.14 inches in that area.
Rocks in lane and heavy rain on SR 330.
SR 163 and Ash street, both lanes on road flooded and vehicles getting stuck.
Mudslide at I-805 north and Sweetwater Rd offramp.
I-5 south and 8th street heavy flooding in lanes 4 and 5 - 0.90 inch rainfall.

Thunderstorm dry microburst. SCE Pack Trail reported gust 41 mph and 92F and 16 percent humidity at 310 pm and CWOP Hesperia reported gust 35 mph at 315 pm. Roof damage reported to a barn by weather spotter.
Home owner shared a video of large hail falling into their pool and patio furniture.
Calle de Los Campesinos road was washed out partially. This road runs along the foothills. Thunderstorms produced very heavy rainfall.
Rocks in the roadway on Pines-to-Palms Highway requiring a plow to remove.
Rocks in both lanes of SR 38. Thunderstorms occurred in this area of Onyx Peak at 8400 feet summit to Baldwin Lake. Caltrans CHP report.
Grapevine Canyon Road, northwest of Big Bear Lake, gust to 48 mph and 63 mph. Sustained wind at 29 mph. 4830 feet MSL. Temperature dropped from 90 to 75.
Flooding and large amount of debris on great southern overland stage and Butterfield Ranch Trail road. Radar shows strong thunderstorms with very heavy rainfall.
At 520 PM on 8/11, CalTrans reported several spots where rocks and mud were all over the road near the intersection of Sunrise Hwy and Kitchen Creek Rd near Mt Laguna.

Anza-Borrego DRC posted a video of flash flooding on X. The post stated that flash flooding occurred in the Split Mountain area, in Anza-Borrego Desert State Park near Fish Creek. The post also said locals reported that roads were washed out further south of the area. Rain gauges in the desert record 0.65 to 0.85 inches of rain and gauges in the mountains reported 0.50 to 1.25 inches of rain in the afternoon and early evening of 8/11/24.
Mesonet station 133SE Santa Margarita Res (SCE1133).
Public reported quarter sized hail south of Peris.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd gst report from 3 ESE Perris. Mesonet station SE943 Perris East (SCE943).
CHP reports numerous trees down in Peris.

Survey from Ellis avenue to Ethanac road in Perris and Romoland showed extensive flooding, mud and standing water. Many trees had large broken branches and some trees uprooted. Home fences were damage including vinyl completed downed. A trampoline was found along the road. Other minor damage noted in yards and property. Socal Edison weather station Vector Sherman road recorded a southeast sustained wind 28 mph and gust to 71 mph at 430 pm Sunday September 8. Temperature dropped from 101 to 74F. There were 6 other weather stations surrounding recorded gusts 48 to 53 mph. No evidence of tornadic winds but rather straight line wet microburst.
Greenspot road, mud and water covering roadway. Elder Gulch reported 0.51 inches in 1 hour from thunderstorm.
Mesonet station SE809 CSU San Bernardino (SCE809).
